summary: See clues file for full details. 18 tossups, 36 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 18 tossups and 36 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: /Replacement: Barry Miles and Peter Orlovsky produced a 1969 folk/jazz album based on the poetry of this man.
examples: /Replacement: Barry Miles and Peter Orlovsky produced a 1969 folk/jazz album based on the poetry of this man.

clues.clue: In one poem, a phrase that links an entities' "buildings" to "judgment" is often read to allude to a creation of this man.
examples: In one poem, a phrase that links an entities' "buildings" to "judgment" is often read to allude to a creation of this man.

clues.clue: The speaker of one poem remembers memories of this man after he "rushed up enchanted." A "tragedy among the scholars of war" is described by a light of this person in one poem.
examples: The speaker of one poem remembers memories of this man after he "rushed up enchanted." A "tragedy among the scholars of war" is described by a light of this person in one poem.

clues.clue: A collection by this author was set to music by (*) Allen Ginsberg after this author apparently appeared to Ginsberg while he was masturbating and read the poem "Ah!
examples: A collection by this author was set to music by (*) Allen Ginsberg after this author apparently appeared to Ginsberg while he was masturbating and read the poem "Ah!

clues.clue: Sunflower." This poet inspired the description of Moloch in "Howl" by creating a figure that embodies law and order and holds an architect's tools named Urizen.
examples: Sunflower." This poet inspired the description of Moloch in "Howl" by creating a figure that embodies law and order and holds an architect's tools named Urizen.

clues.clue: In one section of an epic work by this writer, human bodies are woven by looms in a city built from the primordial Furnaces.
examples: In one section of an epic work by this writer, human bodies are woven by looms in a city built from the primordial Furnaces.

clues.clue: A short poem by this writer follows a woman who "hid in Leutha's (ley-OO-thaz) Vale" and is sexually assaulted before consummating her marriage to Theotormon (thee-oh-TOR-mon).
examples: A short poem by this writer follows a woman who "hid in Leutha's (ley-OO-thaz) Vale" and is sexually assaulted before consummating her marriage to Theotormon (thee-oh-TOR-mon).

clues.clue: This writer stated, "I must create a system or be enslaved by another man's" in an epic poem that describes the city of Golgonooza and the marriage of the muse (+) Enitharmon (ee-nih-THAR-mon).
examples: This writer stated, "I must create a system or be enslaved by another man's" in an epic poem that describes the city of Golgonooza and the marriage of the muse (+) Enitharmon (ee-nih-THAR-mon).

clues.clue: This writer retold his Book of Ahania from the point of view of a fallen smith in his Book of Los (rhymes with "dose").
examples: This writer retold his Book of Ahania from the point of view of a fallen smith in his Book of Los (rhymes with "dose").

clues.clue: Aphorisms like "The fox provides for himself, but God provides for the lion" and "Exuberance is beauty" are included in a prophetic book by this writer that begins with the (*) roaring of Rintrah.
examples: Aphorisms like "The fox provides for himself, but God provides for the lion" and "Exuberance is beauty" are included in a prophetic book by this writer that begins with the (*) roaring of Rintrah.

clues.clue: This writer described how the gods Urthona (ur-THOH-nah), Tharmas, Luvah, and Urizen (you-REE-zen) are emanations of a primordial giant in the book The Four Zoas.
examples: This writer described how the gods Urthona (ur-THOH-nah), Tharmas, Luvah, and Urizen (you-REE-zen) are emanations of a primordial giant in the book The Four Zoas.

clues.clue: A poem named for this person refers to "my Cahier d'un retour au pays natal," and quotes, "Tidd the Cato Street conspirator who wrote: / Sir, I Ham a very Bad Hand at Righting." That Tony Harrison poem is titled for "Not Being" this author, who names the meter in which "Poor Poll" and the poems in The Testament of Beauty were written.
examples: A poem named for this person refers to "my Cahier d'un retour au pays natal," and quotes, "Tidd the Cato Street conspirator who wrote: / Sir, I Ham a very Bad Hand at Righting." That Tony Harrison poem is titled for "Not Being" this author, who names the meter in which "Poor Poll" and the poems in The Testament of Beauty were written.
